*Operating leverage*
High fixed costs вҶ’ small revenue change вҶ’ big profit change. Double-edged.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“– *CASE STUDY*
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
*Federer's 54% rule*
Roger Federer won 80% of Grand Slam matches but only 54% of individual points played. The margin between elite and everyone else is tiny. What separates them: *forgetting the last point and focusing fully on the next.* Emotional reset compounds over time.

*Break-even analysis*
Fixed Costs Г· (Price вҲ’ Variable Cost per unit) = break-even units.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“– *CASE STUDY*
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
*Munger's inversion model*
Charlie Munger doesn't ask 'How do I succeed?' He asks 'What would guarantee failure вҖ” and how do I avoid that?' Inverted thinking cuts through wishful analysis. Most people plan for success. The best also plan against the reasons they'd fail.

*Steve Jobs вҖ” fired, then returned*
In 1985, Apple's board fired the man who started it. Jobs used the freedom of a beginner's mind to build Pixar and NeXT. Apple bought NeXT for $429M, and Jobs came back to save the company. *Losing a title is not losing direction.*

*Bezos's regret minimization*
In 1994, Bezos left a $1M/year Wall Street job to sell books online. His framework: imagine yourself at 80 looking back. Would you regret not trying? He said yes вҖ” and quit the same week. *Fear of failure often disguises itself as prudence.*
